proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_enzalutamide=0.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_abiraterone=0.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_docetaxel=0.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_olaparib=0.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_lu177_psma=0.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab=1 have a higher rate of objective_response than patients with treatment_pembrolizumab=0.

analyses.result_summary: OR rate is 0.3614 with enzalutamide vs 0.1589 without (n=20076 vs 29924). Absolute difference +0.2025, chi2 p<1e-300.
OR rate is 0.2381 with abiraterone vs 0.2411 without. Difference -0.003, NS.
OR rate is 0.2399 with docetaxel vs 0.2404 without. Difference -0.0005, NS.
OR rate is 0.2393 with olaparib vs 0.2403 without. Difference -0.001, NS.
OR rate is 0.2427 with lu177-psma vs 0.2398 without. Difference +0.003, NS.
OR rate is 0.2387 with pembrolizumab vs 0.2403 without. Difference -0.002, NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with mcrpc=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with visceral_mets=0.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Older age_years is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher gleason_score is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: OR rate 0.1535 in mcrpc=1 (n=27481) vs 0.3460 in mcrpc=0. Absolute diff -0.193, chi2 p<1e-300. mcrpc is by far the strongest unfavorable prognostic factor.
OR rate 0.2395 with visceral mets vs 0.2404 without. NS.
Logistic coef=-0.285 per ECOG unit (p=9.5e-73). OR rate decreases monotonically with ECOG.
Logistic coef=+0.0002 per year (p=0.82). No age effect.
Logistic coef=+0.005 per gleason unit (p=0.61). No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have a lower rate of objective_response than patients with msi_high=0.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with psma_high=0.

analyses.result_summary: OR rate 0.1497 with BRCA2 mutation vs 0.2503 without. Diff -0.100, chi2 p=5.4e-56.
OR rate 0.1599 with AR-V7+ vs 0.2604 without. Diff -0.100, chi2 p=1.9e-98.
OR rate 0.1760 with MSI-high vs 0.2422 without. Diff -0.066, chi2 p=3.0e-9.
OR rate 0.2386 with PSMA-high vs 0.2426 without. NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher rate of objective_response.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Greater weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Higher nlr is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.
Lower hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with a lower rate of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic coef=+0.096 per g/dL (p=4.4e-6). Higher albumin → higher OR.
Logistic coef=-5.5e-5 per U/L (p=0.68). No effect of LDH on OR.
Logistic coef=-0.0069 per mg/L (p=5.6e-8). Higher CRP → lower OR.
Logistic coef=-0.031 per %-pt of 6-month weight loss (p=3.4e-29). More weight loss → lower OR.
Logistic coef=-0.0047 per ng/mL (p=1.6e-94). Higher PSA → lower OR.
Logistic coef=-0.0026 per unit (p=0.61). No association.
Logistic coef=-0.004 per g/dL (p=0.51). NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Routine chemistry labs (alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, ast_u_l, alt_u_l, total_bilirubin_mg_dl, creatinine_mg_dl, bun_mg_dl, sodium_meq_l, potassium_meq_l, calcium_mg_dl) are not univariately associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: logit('OR ~ feature') for each chemistry lab
analyses.result_summary: Univariate logistic p-values: alk-phos 0.34, AST 0.22, ALT 0.51, bilirubin 0.18, creatinine 0.72, BUN 0.72, sodium 0.60, potassium 0.13, calcium 0.80. None significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The objective_response benefit from treatment_enzalutamide is smaller (or absent) in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -1.27 (p=1.4e-93). Stratified absolute differences: enza effect +0.251 in AR-V7-, only +0.008 in AR-V7+. Strong qualitative interaction: enzalutamide benefit confined to AR-V7-negative patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The objective_response benefit from treatment_enzalutamide is smaller (or absent) in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients (negative interaction).
The objective_response benefit from treatment_enzalutamide is smaller (or absent) in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -1.14 (p=3.3e-42). Enza absolute effect +0.225 in BRCA2- vs +0.006 in BRCA2+. Enzalutamide benefit absent in BRCA2-mutated patients.
Interaction coef = -1.28 (p=1.8e-19). Enza absolute effect +0.209 in MSI-low vs -0.022 in MSI-high. Enzalutamide benefit absent in MSI-high patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Olaparib produces a larger benefit in objective_response in brca2_mutation=1 patients than in brca2_mutation=0 patients (positive interaction; expected from PARP-inhibitor biology).
Pembrolizumab produces a larger benefit in objective_response in msi_high=1 patients than in msi_high=0 patients (positive interaction; expected from immune-checkpoint biology).
Lu177-PSMA produces a larger benefit in objective_response in psma_high=1 patients than in psma_high=0 patients (positive interaction; expected from radioligand mechanism).
Abiraterone benefit on objective_response is smaller in ar_v7_positive=1 patients than in ar_v7_positive=0 patients (negative interaction; expected from AR-V7 resistance biology).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.289 (p=0.045). Direction is OPPOSITE of expected — olaparib does slightly worse in BRCA2+ patients (rate 0.121 in BRCA2+/olap vs 0.153 in BRCA2+/no-olap). Hypothesis refuted.
Interaction coef = +0.017 (p=0.96). No interaction; hypothesis refuted (pembrolizumab does not selectively benefit MSI-high patients).
Interaction coef = -0.054 (p=0.36). No interaction; hypothesis refuted (Lu177-PSMA does not selectively benefit PSMA-high patients).
Interaction coef = +0.063 (p=0.33). No interaction; abiraterone shows neither benefit nor AR-V7-driven heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the canonical-indication subgroup brca2_mutation=1 AND mcrpc=1, treatment_olaparib increases the rate of objective_response.
In the canonical-indication subgroup msi_high=1 AND mcrpc=1, treatment_pembrolizumab increases the rate of objective_response.
In the canonical-indication subgroup psma_high=1 AND mcrpc=1, treatment_lu177_psma increases the rate of objective_response.
In mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases the rate of objective_response.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on BRCA2+/mcrpc=1 subset by olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA2+/mcrpc=1 (n=2724), OR 0.111 with olaparib vs 0.152 without. Diff -0.041, chi2 p=0.074. Refuted (no benefit; nominal harm trend).

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on MSI+/mcrpc=1 subset by pembrolizumab
analyses.result_summary: Within MSI+/mcrpc=1 (n=839), OR 0.089 with pembro vs 0.161 without. Diff -0.072, chi2 p=0.28. NS; no benefit even in expected indication.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on PSMA+/mcrpc=1 subset by Lu177-PSMA
analyses.result_summary: Within PSMA-high/mcrpc=1 (n=16405), OR 0.159 with Lu177 vs 0.150 without. Diff +0.009, chi2 p=0.25. NS.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on mcrpc=1 subset by docetaxel
analyses.result_summary: Within mcrpc=1, OR 0.153 with docetaxel vs 0.154 without. NS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for all features and treatments in a multivariable logistic regression, treatment_enzalutamide remains a strong positive predictor of objective_response while abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, and pembrolizumab show no significant main effect.

analyses.code: logit('OR ~ all 31 features and treatments (no interactions)')
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable coefficients: enzalutamide +1.20 (p<1e-300); other treatments NS (abi -0.025 p=0.31; doce +0.002 p=0.95; olap +0.026 p=0.49; lu177 +0.029 p=0.35; pembro +0.014 p=0.79). Significant covariates: mcrpc -1.13, ECOG -0.33, BRCA2 -0.74, AR-V7 -0.69, MSI-high -0.43, PSA -0.001, albumin +0.13, weight loss -0.036, CRP -0.007. Pseudo-R2 = 0.124. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among all 25 candidate effect modifiers, the largest interaction with treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is with mcrpc (negative), exceeding even the AR-V7, BRCA2, and MSI-high interactions.

analyses.code: for each feature: logit('OR ~ treatment_enzalutamide * feature'); rank interaction term
analyses.result_summary: Interaction p-values (smallest first): mcrpc coef=-1.98 p~0; AR-V7 coef=-1.24 p=1.4e-93; PSA coef=-0.0087 p=1.2e-72; BRCA2 coef=-1.14 p=3.3e-42; MSI coef=-1.28 p=1.8e-19; ECOG coef=+0.14 p=1.6e-5. Other features all p>0.03. mcrpc is by far the strongest single modifier; PSA's interaction reflects collinearity with mcrpc. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_enzalutamide produces a much larger absolute increase in objective_response in mcrpc=0 patients than in mcrpc=1 patients.

analyses.code: stratified rates and chi-square within mcrpc strata
analyses.result_summary: In mcrpc=0 (hormone-sensitive, n=22519): enza+ rate 0.610 vs enza- 0.169, diff +0.440. In mcrpc=1 (CRPC, n=27481): enza+ rate 0.158 vs enza- 0.151, diff +0.008. Effect concentrated entirely in non-CRPC patients. Hypothesis strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment_enzalutamide produces a clinically meaningful increase in objective_response only in patients who simultaneously have mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; in all other strata the effect is null.

analyses.result_summary: Joint logistic regression: enza main effect = +2.58; all four enza-by-modifier interactions strongly negative (mcrpc -2.20 p<1e-300; AR-V7 -1.61 p=4e-136; BRCA2 -1.52 p=2e-65; MSI -1.65 p=4e-27). Pseudo-R2 = 0.166 (vs 0.067 main-effects-only enza model). Each modifier contributes independently — they do not redundantly proxy each other. Strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining favorable_for_enza := (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0) cleanly captures the entire enzalutamide-responsive subgroup: enzalutamide raises objective_response by ≥0.5 absolute inside this stratum and by essentially zero outside it.

analyses.code: stratified 2x2 tables and logit('OR ~ enza * favorable_for_enza')
analyses.result_summary: favorable_for_enza covers 31.4% of cohort (n=15681). Inside favorable subgroup: enza+ rate 0.798 (n=6325) vs enza- 0.172 (n=9356), absolute diff +0.626, chi2 p<1e-300. Outside subgroup (n=34319): enza+ rate 0.161 vs enza- 0.153, diff +0.008, chi2 p=0.056 (likely chance). Logit interaction enza:favorable = +2.89 (p<1e-300). Strongly supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each of the 4 cells of (mcrpc × any-resistance-marker), only the (mcrpc=0, no-resistance) cell shows an enzalutamide treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: mcrpc=0/no_resist=1: enza+ 0.798 vs enza- 0.172 (+0.626, n=15681). mcrpc=0/no_resist=0: enza+ 0.171 vs enza- 0.163 (+0.008, n=6838). mcrpc=1/no_resist=1: enza+ 0.159 vs enza- 0.152 (+0.007, n=19163). mcrpc=1/no_resist=0: enza+ 0.155 vs enza- 0.147 (+0.008, n=8318). Effect uniquely concentrated in (mcrpc=0, no-resistance) cell. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable enzalutamide subgroup, the absolute objective_response benefit from enzalutamide is preserved across ECOG levels 0, 1, and 2 (≥+0.55 in each) although somewhat attenuated at ECOG=2.

analyses.code: stratified rates by ECOG within favorable subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Within favorable_for_enza: ECOG 0 enza+ 0.859 vs enza- 0.213 (diff +0.646); ECOG 1 enza+ 0.781 vs enza- 0.159 (diff +0.622); ECOG 2 enza+ 0.708 vs enza- 0.119 (diff +0.590). Benefit very large at every PS, modestly attenuated by worsening PS. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher log(1+psa_ng_ml) further attenuates the enzalutamide effect on objective_response even after adjusting for mcrpc and the resistance markers.

analyses.code: logit('OR ~ enza * log1p(psa) + mcrpc + ar_v7 + brca2 + msi')
analyses.result_summary: Interaction enza:log_psa = -0.474 (p=4e-132), even after adjusting for mcrpc, AR-V7, BRCA2, MSI. PSA captures additional disease-burden information that further reduces enzalutamide benefit. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding abiraterone to enzalutamide does not increase the objective_response rate compared with enzalutamide alone (no enza+abi synergy).

analyses.result_summary: Rates: enza-/abi- 0.160; enza-/abi+ 0.157; enza+/abi- 0.363; enza+/abi+ 0.357. Abiraterone neither helps alone nor adds to enzalutamide. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: None of treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, treatment_pembrolizumab show a Bonferroni-significant interaction with any of the 25 candidate features on objective_response.

analyses.code: for each non-enza treatment × each feature: logit interaction; check Bonferroni threshold 0.05/(5×25)=4e-4
analyses.result_summary: Best p-values per drug: abiraterone × BRCA2 p=0.031; docetaxel × sodium p=0.023; olaparib × BUN p=0.031; lu177 × visceral_mets p=0.010; pembrolizumab × gleason p=0.033. None survive Bonferroni correction (threshold 4e-4). No reliable subgroup of efficacy detected for these five drugs. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: visceral_mets does not modify the enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
albumin_g_dl does not meaningfully modify the enzalutamide effect on objective_response.
psma_high does not modify the enzalutamide effect on objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coef = -0.009 (p=0.86). No modification.
Interaction coef = -0.082 (p=0.058). Borderline; not robust.
Interaction coef = +0.037 (p=0.40). No modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all six treatments, no treatment except enzalutamide shows a clinically meaningful subgroup in which it improves objective_response by ≥0.05 absolute and reaches statistical significance.

analyses.code: Combined evidence: stratified subgroup tests (h9), interaction screens (h19), main-effect multivariable model (h10).
analyses.result_summary: Direct subgroup tests (canonical biomarker × mcrpc cells) and adjusted multivariable estimates show no positive efficacy signal for abiraterone, docetaxel, olaparib, lu177_psma, or pembrolizumab. Combined main-effect estimates straddle zero with adjusted ORs of ~1 (logits between -0.03 and +0.03). Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within any biomarker stratum (BRCA2, AR-V7, MSI, PSMA, mcrpc, visceral_mets), treatment_docetaxel shows no >+0.05 absolute increase in objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Across strata, docetaxel-treated and untreated arms differ by <0.02 absolute everywhere. No subgroup showing benefit identified. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable enzalutamide subgroup, declining albumin and rising PSA, CRP, and weight loss each independently lower objective_response, even on enzalutamide.

analyses.code: logit('OR ~ enza + favorable_for_enza + enza:favorable_for_enza + ECOG + visc + albumin + crp + weight_loss + log(PSA)')
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted multivariable model: ECOG -0.38 p<1e-90; albumin +0.15 p=2e-9; CRP -0.0085 p=8e-9; weight loss -0.042 p<1e-30; log(PSA) -0.088 p=2e-15; visceral_mets NS. The enza:favorable interaction term remains +2.95 (p<1e-300). Disease-burden markers add to but do not eliminate the favorable-subgroup × enzalutamide effect. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding any of (visceral_mets, ECOG, age, gleason, hemoglobin, NLR, LDH, alkaline_phosphatase, AST, ALT, bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, Na, K, Ca) to the enzalutamide × (mcrpc, ar_v7, brca2, msi) subgroup definition does NOT identify any additional variable whose unfavorable level qualitatively suppresses the enzalutamide effect (i.e., reduces it from large to null) within the otherwise-favorable subgroup.

analyses.code: Within favorable_for_enza=1 cohort, fit logit('OR ~ enza * extra') for each extra; check whether enza:extra interaction reverses or eliminates the +0.6 effect.
analyses.result_summary: Stratified analyses show enza effect ≥+0.55 absolute at every ECOG level (0,1,2), with and without visceral mets, across age tertiles, and across labs in clinically reasonable ranges. No additional variable qualitatively eliminates the enzalutamide effect within the (mcrpc=0, ar_v7-, brca2-, msi-) subgroup. The 4-way subgroup definition is therefore a complete predicate for enzalutamide responsiveness in this cohort. Supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL CONSOLIDATED HYPOTHESIS: treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response by approximately +0.63 absolute (from 0.17 to 0.80) ONLY in the subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0; outside this subgroup the enzalutamide effect on objective_response is essentially 0 (≈+0.008). No other treatment in this dataset shows a clinically meaningful efficacy signal in any subgroup.

analyses.code: logit('OR ~ enza * favorable_for_enza') and direct stratified estimates
analyses.result_summary: Inside favorable_for_enza (n=15681; 31.4% of cohort): OR rate 0.798 on enza vs 0.172 off, +0.626 absolute (p<1e-300). Outside favorable_for_enza (n=34319): 0.161 on vs 0.153 off, +0.008 (p=0.056). Joint logit interaction enza:favorable_for_enza = +2.89 (z=56). All four predicates (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7=0 AND brca2=0 AND msi=0) are required — relaxing any one predicate eliminates the enzalutamide treatment effect in the resulting cell. The unfavorable values that suppress the treatment effect are: mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, msi_high=1. Strongly supported.
